name: Sunscreen
summary: Sunscreen is normalized as the comparator and combination component in topical DNA-repair enzyme skin studies.
mechanism_summary: Reduces UV exposure to prevent DNA damage in skin, with linked records focused on local CPD and field-cancerization endpoints.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: Sunscreen plus DNA repair enzymes in actinic keratosis; Sunscreen plus DNA repair enzymes lowered CPDs and field cancerization more than sunscreen alone.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
